connection: plain form / N + とはいえ
nuance: Concedes the first clause as true, then pivots — 'granted X, still Y'. Formal, common in writing and measured speech.

examples.en: I've gotten used to Tokyo — that said, the clammy humidity of the rainy season is the one thing I still can't stand.
The contract was extended; nevertheless, my visa's expiry date doesn't change.
Used to it as I am, I still can't stand packed trains.

caution: Don't confuse it with とはいうものの (same meaning, more spoken): とはいえ can also open a sentence as a connector, whereas ものの cannot stand alone.

peg.ja: 旅は終わったとはいえ、思い出は消えない。
peg.romaji: Tabi wa owatta to wa ie, omoide wa kienai.
peg.en: The journey may be over — that said, the memories won't fade.
peg.source: 葬送のフリーレン — フリーレン

connection: N / plain form + といえども
nuance: Literary 'even for X / even though'. Concedes an extreme or authoritative case, then denies it holds. Stiffer and older than とはいえ.

examples.en: Even a veteran can't take a break during the year-end rush.
Even a single person is not permitted to disturb the harmony of the workplace.
Even a veteran must not let their guard down.

caution: It's archaic — reserve it for formal writing and proverbs; using it in everyday conversation sounds stiff and theatrical where とはいえ or ても would be natural.

peg.ja: 英雄といえども、時には迷う。
peg.romaji: Eiyū to iedomo, toki ni wa mayou.
peg.en: Even a hero, at times, wavers.
peg.source: 鬼滅の刃 — 産屋敷耀哉

connection: plain form / N + どころか
nuance: Corrects an expectation, often to its opposite: 'far from X — in fact the reverse'. Can also stack a stronger case ('let alone').

examples.en: Far from saving money, I'm in the red this month.
Let alone a break — I didn't even have time to eat lunch.
Far from getting easier, the work just keeps increasing.

caution: The second clause must be stronger or opposite to the first — pairing どころか with a merely similar or weaker statement is wrong; that's what makes it 'far from', not plain 'and also'.

peg.ja: 許すどころか、俺はお前を倒す。
peg.romaji: Yurusu dokoro ka, ore wa omae o taosu.
peg.en: Far from forgiving you, I'll destroy you.
peg.source: ドラゴンボール — 孫悟空

connection: V-dict / N + どころではない
nuance: The situation makes X out of the question — there's no room for it. Emphatic dismissal driven by circumstance.

examples.en: With the moving preparations, this is no time for sightseeing.
I had a fever, so it was no time for going to see the cherry blossoms.
Before the deadline, this is no time for a lunch break.

caution: Don't mix it up with どころか: どころではない says circumstances rule the action out ('no time for it'), while どころか corrects a factual expectation ('far from').

peg.ja: 休んでいるどころではない。
peg.romaji: Yasunde iru dokoro de wa nai.
peg.en: This is no time to be resting.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — エレン

nuance: Two events almost overlap — the second follows the first with no gap. Literary; describes an observed, often surprising, immediacy.

examples.en: The instant the last train's doors opened, the passengers poured in.
The instant my boss sat down, he started replying to emails.
The instant he left the ticket gate, he broke into a run.

caution: The main clause must be past tense and describe an actual observed event — you can't use が早いか for a command, intention, or future/habitual statement.

peg.ja: 刀を抜くが早いか、鬼の首は落ちていた。
peg.romaji: Katana o nuku ga hayai ka, oni no kubi wa ochite ita.
peg.en: The instant he drew his sword, the demon's head had fallen.
peg.source: 鬼滅の刃 — 冨岡義勇

nuance: The second action erupts the moment the first ends. Very literary; the two are all but simultaneous.

examples.en: No sooner had I stepped out of the ticket gate than the rain started.
No sooner had I sent the email than a reply came back.
No sooner had the bell rung than the students dashed out of the classroom.

caution: Like が早いか, the second clause states something that actually happened (past tense) — や否や can't take a volitional, request, or negative main clause.

peg.ja: 合図が出るや否や、全隊が動いた。
peg.romaji: Aizu ga deru ya ina ya, zentai ga ugoita.
peg.en: No sooner was the signal given than the whole corps moved.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — エルヴィン

nuance: The same person does the second action the moment the first ends — usually an unexpected or vivid follow-up. Literary.

examples.en: My colleague got home and, the moment he did, collapsed onto the futon.
The child saw the souvenir and instantly let out a cheer.
The moment my boss took his seat, he began making phone calls.

caution: Unlike が早いか and や否や, なり requires the SAME subject for both actions and a past-tense main clause; using it with two different subjects is the classic error.

peg.ja: 彼は剣を取るなり、闇へと駆けた。
peg.romaji: Kare wa ken o toru nari, yami e to kaketa.
peg.en: He seized his sword and, the moment he did, dashed into the dark.
peg.source: 鬼滅の刃 — 煉獄杏寿郎

nuance: Alongside one's main occupation, one also does X over a long span. Formal; the side activity is a sustained second pursuit, not a one-off.

examples.en: Alongside working at an izakaya, I keep up my study of Japanese.
On top of her office job, she teaches pottery on weekends.
Alongside working at a company, I teach Japanese.

caution: It's for a sustained long-term second pursuit alongside a main one — don't use かたわら for a one-off dual-purpose errand; that's がてら's job.

peg.ja: 戦いのかたわら、彼は仲間を守り続けた。
peg.romaji: Tatakai no katawara, kare wa nakama o mamoritsuzuketa.
peg.en: Alongside fighting, he kept protecting his comrades.
peg.source: 鋼の錬金術師 — ロイ・マスタング

nuance: One outing serves two purposes at once — do A and, in the same trip, also do B. Casual-to-neutral; a single occasion, not a long-term habit.

examples.en: On my walk, I dropped by the neighborhood shrine while I was at it.
While out shopping, I dropped off some paperwork at the ward office too.
On a walk, I dropped by the local bathhouse while I was at it.

caution: がてら is a single occasion combining two purposes on the same outing — don't stretch it to a long-running side occupation (use かたわら), and it attaches to motion-related nouns/stems.

peg.ja: 散歩がてら、様子を見てきた。
peg.romaji: Sanpo gatera, yōsu o mite kita.
peg.en: On a walk, I checked things out while I was at it.
peg.source: 銀魂 — 坂田銀時

connection: N/V + にしろ / にせよ
nuance: Lists alternatives and says the conclusion holds for all of them. にしろ and にせよ are interchangeable and often paired for balance; formal.

examples.en: Whether I stay or go back home, I should decide now.
Whether full-time or part-time work, you pay taxes either way.
Whether you go or not, please let me know early.

caution: The paired items should be genuine alternatives (often opposites); don't attach な before a noun — it's N + にしろ directly, e.g. 学生にしろ, not 学生なにしろ.

peg.ja: 進むにしろ退くにせよ、覚悟を決めろ。
peg.romaji: Susumu ni shiro shirizoku ni seyo, kakugo o kimero.
peg.en: Whether you advance or retreat, steel yourself.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — リヴァイ

nuance: 'No matter what/who/where' — the following holds regardless. Often after a question word (誰, どこ, どんな). Formal, decisive.

examples.en: No matter the weather, delivery work can't take a day off.
Be it any foreigner, you must always carry your residence card.
Be it any job, I give it my all.

caution: Attach であれ directly to a noun (誰であれ), not to a な-adjective's な form — and remember it needs a following clause that holds unconditionally, not a simple 'even if' aside.

peg.ja: 何者であれ、我が前に立つ者は退ける。
peg.romaji: Nanimono de are, waga mae ni tatsu mono wa shirizokeru.
peg.en: Be it anyone, whoever stands before me I cast aside.
peg.source: コードギアス — ルルーシュ

connection: N + といい + N + といい
nuance: Cites two representative aspects to back an overall judgment — 'what with X and with Y (, it's...)'. Evaluative; the speaker is impressed or dismayed.

examples.en: What with the price and with the location, this share house is ideal.
What with the autumn leaves and with the air, Kyoto in fall is exceptional.
What with the taste and the price, this set meal is beyond complaint.

caution: Both slots take plain nouns (N といい N といい) and lead to an overall evaluation — don't use it for a neutral list; if you're not passing judgment, use も…も instead.

peg.ja: 剣といい心といい、あの男は本物だ。
peg.romaji: Ken to ii kokoro to ii, ano otoko wa honmono da.
peg.en: What with his blade and his heart, that man is the real thing.
peg.source: 鬼滅の刃 — 煉獄杏寿郎

connection: N + といわず + N + といわず
nuance: Names two parts only to say the whole is affected — 'not just A, not just B, but all over'. Literary, emphatic totality.

examples.en: Not just my hands, not just my face — the pollen makes me itch all over.
Weekday or weekend, no exceptions — Shibuya is always packed.
Walls, floor, no exception — the whole room was covered in dust.

caution: The two nouns are just examples standing for the whole ('all over'), so don't read といわず as literal negation ('not saying') — and it takes nouns, not verbs.

peg.ja: 昼といわず夜といわず、奴は現れる。
peg.romaji: Hiru to iwazu yoru to iwazu, yatsu wa arawareru.
peg.en: Day or night, no exception — he appears.
peg.source: 東京喰種 — 亜門鋼太朗

nuance: 'X goes without saying, and even Y as well.' Formal written equivalent of はもちろん; adds a further, often stronger, case.

examples.en: Kanji not to mention, even honorific speech was drilled into me at work.
Tokyo goes without saying — I'd also like to tour the festivals out in the regions.
Weekends go without saying — weekdays too are fully booked.

caution: It only adds a further item ('not to mention'); don't use はもとより to mean 'from the start / originally' — that sense is the separate adverb もともと.

peg.ja: 剣術はもとより、兵法にも通じている。
peg.romaji: Kenjutsu wa motoyori, heihō ni mo tsūjite iru.
peg.en: Swordsmanship goes without saying — he's versed in strategy too.
peg.source: るろうに剣心 — 比古清十郎

nuance: Praises something as achievable by X alone — 'distinctive of X'. Almost always positive; often 〜ならではの + noun.

examples.en: I encountered the human warmth unique to the old-town district.
The snowy scenery you can only get in winter Hokkaido is unforgettable.
I'm completely hooked on the flavor unique to this shop.

caution: When a noun follows, you must insert の — ならではの + noun (下町ならではの人情); dropping the の (下町ならでは人情) is the classic mistake, and it stays positive.

peg.ja: 彼ならではの一撃だった。
peg.romaji: Kare naradewa no ichigeki datta.
peg.en: It was a strike only he could deliver.
peg.source: ハイキュー!! — 及川徹

nuance: Acts without regard for X — often others' concern, warnings, or fuss. Faintly critical; literary.

examples.en: Ignoring the typhoon warning, he went in to the office.
Indifferent to my family's worry, I stayed abroad on my own.
Ignoring the traffic jam, he strolled there leisurely.

caution: Unlike neutral にもかかわらず, をよそに carries a critical nuance of willfully disregarding someone's concern or a warning — don't use it for a neutral 'despite'.

peg.ja: 周囲の制止をよそに、彼は前へ出た。
peg.romaji: Shūi no seishi o yoso ni, kare wa mae e deta.
peg.en: Ignoring everyone's restraint, he stepped forward.
peg.source: 鬼滅の刃 — 竈門炭治郎

nuance: Stiff, ceremonial 'as of / by means of'. Common in official notices — a date marks a boundary, or a means accomplishes an end.

examples.en: This contract will conclude as of the last day of June.
Identity is confirmed by means of an ID document.
As of today, our store will close.

caution: The 'as of (a date/time)' boundary sense and the 'by means of' instrument sense are different — for a concrete physical tool prefer で; をもって is for ceremonial dates or abstract means.

peg.ja: 我が全霊をもって、これを断つ。
peg.romaji: Waga zenrei o motte, kore o tatsu.
peg.en: With my whole soul, I sever this.
peg.source: ジョジョの奇妙な冒険 — DIO

nuance: Reaching an extreme or critical stage before something finally happens — often 'only after it got this bad did they act'.

examples.en: Only when it came to my resignation did the company finally begin to consider improving conditions.
Only when my savings had run dry did I finally become conscious of frugality.
Only when moving-out day came did I realize how hard the cleaning would be.

caution: Don't confuse に至って (finally reaching an extreme stage before acting) with に至るまで (spanning a range down to even the smallest item) — they are different patterns.

peg.ja: 破滅に至って、彼はようやく悟った。
peg.romaji: Hametsu ni itatte, kare wa yōyaku satotta.
peg.en: Only when it came to ruin did he finally understand.
peg.source: コードギアス — ルルーシュ

nuance: Emphasizes an extreme endpoint of a range — 'from A all the way down to even B'. Stresses thoroughness.

examples.en: At immigration screening, everything down to the cash I was carrying was checked.
In the share house, rules are set in detail for everything from shared facilities down to the seasonings.
In the interview, I paid attention to everything from clothing down to word choice.

caution: It marks the extreme endpoint of an exhaustive range ('down to even'), not a time deadline — for 'up until (a time)' use plain まで, not に至るまで.

peg.ja: 髪の一本に至るまで、俺は見逃さない。
peg.romaji: Kami no ippon ni itaru made, ore wa minogasanai.
peg.en: Down to a single strand of hair, I miss nothing.
peg.source: デスノート — L

nuance: The first item that launches a spreading series — one event kicks off a run of similar ones.

examples.en: Starting with the Tokyo performance, the nationwide tour began.
Starting with Sapporo's Snow Festival, winter events continue in various places.
Starting with the interview, my job hunt began in earnest.

caution: The starting event must launch an expanding, ongoing series (a tour, a chain of openings) — don't use を皮切りに for a one-off first step that leads nowhere further.

peg.ja: その一撃を皮切りに、反撃が始まった。
peg.romaji: Sono ichigeki o kawakiri ni, hangeki ga hajimatta.
peg.en: Starting with that one blow, the counterattack began.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — アルミン

nuance: Formal 'at the time of / on the occasion of' a notable event — used in speeches and announcements.

examples.en: On the occasion of moving in, registration of a guarantor is required.
On the occasion of renewing the contract, a handling fee is paid.
On the occasion of entry, a residence card was issued.

caution: に際して is for a specific one-time occasion or event, not an ongoing condition — and it's more solemn than plain とき; don't use it for everyday situations.

peg.ja: 出撃に際して、一つだけ言っておく。
peg.romaji: Shutsugeki ni saishite, hitotsu dake itte oku.
peg.en: On the occasion of this sortie, I'll say one thing.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — エルヴィン

nuance: Formal 'at the important juncture of' — used before a significant undertaking, with a positive, deliberate tone.

examples.en: At the important time of coming to Japan, I prepared to open a bank account.
At the important time of starting a new life, I set my goals.
At the time of renewing the contract, I checked the required documents.

caution: Reserve にあたって for a positive, significant undertaking one is about to begin — using it for a trivial or negative event sounds off; and it points forward, not backward.

peg.ja: 旅立ちにあたって、師は一言だけ残した。
peg.romaji: Tabidachi ni atatte, shi wa hitokoto dake nokoshita.
peg.en: At the moment of departure, the master left just one word.
peg.source: NARUTO -ナルト- — 自来也

nuance: Formal 'in advance of' a significant event — the preparation that precedes it, more deliberate than plain 前に.

examples.en: Prior to the store opening, a preview event was held.
Prior to the interview, I submit my résumé in advance.
Prior to construction, a briefing for residents was held.

caution: に先立って means preparation done in advance of a big event, so the two clauses share a topic — don't use it for two unrelated sequential actions where 前に would do.

peg.ja: 決戦に先立って、彼らは誓いを立てた。
peg.romaji: Kessen ni sakidatte, karera wa chikai o tateta.
peg.en: Prior to the final battle, they swore an oath.
peg.source: 鬼滅の刃 — 産屋敷耀哉

nuance: 'Taking X as a basis / footing' — a decision or argument grounded on established facts or prior results.

examples.en: Building on the interview results, the hiring decision is made.
Taking the local cost of living into account, I revised my budget.
Building on last year's reflection, I made this year's plan.

caution: を踏まえて takes established facts/results as a basis for what follows — don't confuse it with を込めて ('putting feeling into'); the noun before it must be information, not emotion.

peg.ja: これまでの情報を踏まえて、次の一手を打つ。
peg.romaji: Kore made no jōhō o fumaete, tsugi no itte o utsu.
peg.en: Building on the intel so far, I make the next move.
peg.source: デスノート — 夜神月

nuance: Conforming strictly to facts, rules, or reality — 'in line with', not deviating from the concrete situation.

examples.en: In accordance with the terms, I proceed with the move-out procedure.
You should draw up the plan in accordance with your actual income.
In accordance with company regulations, I file the expenses.

caution: に即して conforms to concrete facts, rules, or reality — don't swap it for に沿って, which follows an abstract line/policy/wish; 即して needs a factual, rule-like standard.

peg.ja: 掟に即して、裁きを下す。
peg.romaji: Okite ni sokushite, sabaki o kudasu.
peg.en: In accordance with the code, I pass judgment.
peg.source: PSYCHO-PASS — 狡噛慎也

nuance: 'Under' someone's supervision or a set of conditions — the influence or environment in which something happens.

examples.en: Under a senior colleague's guidance, I learn customer service.
Under the conditions of a limited budget, I plan the trip.
Under a strict boss, I learned a great deal.

caution: のもとで marks the conditions/supervision under which something happens; don't confuse it with のもとに, which more often frames a premise or principle ('on the basis of').

peg.ja: あの人のもとで、俺は強くなった。
peg.romaji: Ano hito no moto de, ore wa tsuyoku natta.
peg.en: Under that person, I grew strong.
peg.source: 鋼の錬金術師 — エドワード

nuance: 'Not asking about X' — X makes no difference. Often paired with a range noun like 年齢 or 経験.

examples.en: Regardless of whether you have experience, you can apply.
Regardless of nationality or age, anyone can take part.
Regardless of experience, we welcome motivated applicants.

caution: It pairs with a noun naming a category of variation (年齢を問わず, 経験を問わず) — don't attach を問わず to a full yes/no clause; for that use かどうかにかかわらず.

peg.ja: 才能の有無を問わず、道は開ける。
peg.romaji: Sainō no umu o towazu, michi wa hirakeru.
peg.en: Regardless of talent, the path can open.
peg.source: ブラッククローバー — アスタ

connection: N / V-dict + にかかわらず
nuance: X does not affect the outcome — often with paired opposites (多い少ない, 晴雨) or a yes/no verb pair.

examples.en: Regardless of whether the weather is good or bad, the fireworks event goes ahead.
Whether or not you attend, please send a reply.
Regardless of the amount, I keep every receipt.

caution: Don't confuse にかかわらず ('regardless of') with にもかかわらず ('despite the fact that') — the も version concedes a surprising contrast, the plain version dismisses a variable.

peg.ja: 勝敗にかかわらず、全力を尽くせ。
peg.romaji: Shōhai ni kakawarazu, zenryoku o tsukuse.
peg.en: Regardless of victory or defeat, give it your all.
peg.source: ハイキュー!! — 烏養繋心

nuance: Very formal 'depends on the nature of X' — the outcome hinges on X. 如何 is usually written in kana.

examples.en: Whether you're hired depends on how the interview goes.
Whether the visa can be extended depends on the person's own effort.
Whether the contract is renewed depends on your performance.

caution: いかんによっては/いかんで means 'depending on X', but いかんによらず/いかんにかかわらず flips to 'regardless of X' — mixing the two up reverses the meaning entirely.

peg.ja: 作戦の成否は、お前の判断いかんだ。
peg.romaji: Sakusen no seihi wa, omae no handan ikan da.
peg.en: The operation's success hinges on your judgment.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — エルヴィン

nuance: Smeared all over with something unpleasant — mud, sweat, dust, blood, debt. Negative, physical nuance.

examples.en: I got covered in sweat from the work of moving house.
Don't end up buried in debt from reckless spending.
I fell off my bike and my hands got covered in mud.

caution: まみれ only attaches to a clinging physical substance (泥, 汗, 血, 借金) — don't use it for abstract states or a mere abundance of good things; that's ずくめ or だらけ territory.

peg.ja: 血まみれになっても、彼は刃を離さなかった。
peg.romaji: Chi mamire ni natte mo, kare wa yaiba o hanasanakatta.
peg.en: Even covered in blood, he never let go of his blade.
peg.source: 鬼滅の刃 — 竈門炭治郎

nuance: Entirely made up of one thing — an unbroken run of it (good news, black clothing, rules). Neutral-to-positive.

examples.en: This month was nothing but good news.
The ceremony's attendees were dressed in nothing but black.
This week was nothing but overtime, and I'm exhausted.

caution: ずくめ is 'entirely composed of' one thing (often positive: いいことずくめ), while だらけ is 'full of' scattered unwanted things (間違いだらけ) — don't treat them as synonyms.

peg.ja: 今日は悪いことずくめだ。
peg.romaji: Kyō wa warui koto zukume da.
peg.en: Today has been nothing but bad news.
peg.source: 名探偵コナン — 江戸川コナン

connection: N + めく (春めく→春めいた)
nuance: Verb suffix meaning 'to take on the air/signs of' — 春めく = to grow spring-like. Conjugates as a う-verb.

examples.en: March came, and the air grew spring-like.
I got irritated at his sarcasm-tinged way of speaking.
October came, and the town has taken on an autumn air.

caution: めく is a verb suffix, so conjugate it (春めく→春めいた, 謎めいている) — treating it as a noun or na-adjective is wrong; and it means 'take on the air of', not literal becoming.

peg.ja: 春めく風の中で、彼女は静かに笑った。
peg.romaji: Harumeku kaze no naka de, kanojo wa shizuka ni waratta.
peg.en: In the spring-like breeze, she smiled quietly.
peg.source: 葬送のフリーレン — フリーレン

meaning: (hope/wish) from the bottom of one's heart
nuance: A ceaselessly-held feeling — the emotion (hope, love, respect) never stops. Literary, reserved for strong sincere sentiment.

examples.en: I wish from the bottom of my heart for a safe journey to Japan.
I pray with all my heart for the success of my new life here.
I wish for my hometown friends' happiness with all my heart.

caution: It only attaches to a small set of emotion verbs of hope/love/respect (願う, 愛する, 尊敬する) — don't use てやまない with ordinary action verbs.

peg.ja: 君の無事を、願ってやまない。
peg.romaji: Kimi no buji o, negatte yamanai.
peg.en: I wish for your safety from the bottom of my heart.
peg.source: 鋼の錬金術師 — ロイ・マスタング

nuance: Two senses: with a verb, cannot bear to (見るに堪えない); with an emotion noun, filled to overflowing with (感謝に堪えない).

examples.en: The damage from the typhoon is too painful to look at.
I am overwhelmed with gratitude for everyone's support.
I cannot express enough gratitude for everyone's kindness.

caution: Keep the two senses apart: with a perception verb it's 'cannot bear to' (見るに堪えない), but with an emotion noun it's 'overflowing with' (感謝に堪えない) — the noun/verb choice flips the meaning.

peg.ja: その光景は、見るに堪えなかった。
peg.romaji: Sono kōkei wa, miru ni taenakatta.
peg.en: That sight was too painful to look at.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — アルミン

nuance: The speaker's own feeling pushed to its limit — nothing could be more X. Only for one's own emotion, never a third party's.

examples.en: Spending New Year's alone is lonely beyond words.
My visa came through without a hitch — I couldn't be happier.
I got the job offer I'd longed for — I couldn't be happier.

caution: 限りだ only follows an emotion adjective describing the speaker's own feeling (嬉しい限りだ, 心細い限りだ) — don't use it with a plain noun or for a third party's emotion.

peg.ja: 一人残されて、心細い限りだ。
peg.romaji: Hitori nokosarete, kokorobosoi kagiri da.
peg.en: Left all alone, I'm lonely beyond words.
peg.source: 鋼の錬金術師 — アルフォンス

nuance: A bad process leads to an even worse final state — 'and in the end, this is what happened'. Always negative, often exasperated.

examples.en: The overtime kept piling up, and I ended up missing the last train.
I got lost, and in the end I had no choice but to cancel the reservation.
I got lost repeatedly and ended up being late after all.

caution: 始末だ always caps a deteriorating process with a deplorable outcome — don't use it for a neutral or good result; the whole build-up must be negative.

peg.ja: 慢心した挙げ句、敗れる始末だ。
peg.romaji: Manshin shita ageku, yabureru shimatsu da.
peg.en: Grown overconfident, he ended up defeated.
peg.source: ハンター×ハンター — ネテロ

meaning: merely / if need be I'll just
nuance: With V-dict: a calm resolve — if it comes to that, I'll simply do X, no big deal. With V-た: I merely did X, nothing more was meant by it.

examples.en: If I miss the last train, I'll just walk home, that's all.
I merely told you how I honestly feel — nothing more.
Even if I fail, I'll just do it over, that's all.

caution: Watch the tense: V-dict + までだ is resolve ('I'll just do X if it comes to it'), but V-た + までだ is 'I merely did X (no more was meant)' — the plain/past form changes the meaning.

peg.ja: やられたら、やり返すまでだ。
peg.romaji: Yararetara, yarikaesu made da.
peg.en: If I'm struck, I simply strike back, that's all.
peg.source: コードギアス — ルルーシュ

meaning: no need to go so far as
nuance: The action is so obvious or minor that taking that step would be excessive — no need to bother.

examples.en: It's close enough that there's no need to bother taking the train.
It goes without saying, but always carry your residence card on you.
It's close, so there's no need to go so far as to call a taxi.

caution: It attaches to the dictionary form of a verb (言うまでもない), not the ます-stem or a noun — and it means 'no need to bother', not 'not allowed to'.

peg.ja: 言うまでもない。俺が勝つ。
peg.romaji: Iu made mo nai. Ore ga katsu.
peg.en: It goes without saying — I win.
peg.source: ジョジョの奇妙な冒険 — 空条承太郎

nuance: Classical prohibition seen on signs and notices. Suru takes する→すべからず (or classical せ). Blunt and impersonal.

examples.en: No dumping of trash here. (sign)
No entry without permission. (sign)
Keep off the grass. (sign)

caution: べからず is signs-and-notices only (立入るべからず) and blunt/impersonal — never use it as a spoken prohibition to a person; and する becomes すべからず (or classical せ), not するべからず in set phrases.

peg.ja: この地に、何人も立ち入るべからず。
peg.romaji: Kono chi ni, nanpito mo tachiiru bekarazu.
peg.en: Into this land, no one shall set foot.
peg.source: 犬夜叉 — 奈落

connection: V-dict + べく (する→すべく) (するべく also attested)
nuance: A literary 'in order to / with the aim of'. Suru contracts to すべく. Used in formal writing, not casual speech.

examples.en: In order to renew my visa, I headed for the ward office.
In order to prepare for the typhoon, I stocked up on food supplies.
In order to fulfill his dream, he moved to Tokyo.

caution: べく cannot be followed by ください or any request/command — it's a literary 'in order to' that leads into a statement; and する contracts to すべく.

peg.ja: 真実を暴くべく、俺はここに来た。
peg.romaji: Shinjitsu o abaku beku, ore wa koko ni kita.
peg.en: In order to uncover the truth, I came here.
peg.source: PSYCHO-PASS — 狡噛慎也

nuance: Casual, emphatic denial — there's absolutely no chance of it happening. Attaches to the masu-stem. Conversational only.

examples.en: There's no way I can pay rent that high.
Even if we leave now, there's no way we'll make the last train.
There's no way I can memorize kanji in one night.

caution: っこない attaches to the ます-stem (できっこない, 分かりっこない), never the dictionary form — and it's strictly casual, so avoid it in formal or written contexts.

peg.ja: 諦めるなんて、できっこない。
peg.romaji: Akirameru nante, dekikko nai.
peg.en: Giving up? No way I could.
peg.source: 僕のヒーローアカデミア — 緑谷出久

connection: V-ない + では済まない
nuance: Given the circumstances, one won't be let off without doing X — social or situational pressure makes it unavoidable. ずには済まない is a stiffer variant.

examples.en: Since I broke the contract, I won't get away without paying a penalty.
On the noise issue, I won't get away without apologizing to the neighbor.
Since the documents had errors, I won't get away without redoing them.

caution: It means social/situational pressure makes X unavoidable ('won't be let off without'), not personal inability — don't confuse it with なければならない, a plain obligation.

peg.ja: この落とし前、つけないでは済まない。
peg.romaji: Kono otoshimae, tsukenaide wa sumanai.
peg.en: This score won't get settled without payback.
peg.source: ワンピース — トラファルガー・ロー

connection: N + を余儀なくされる
nuance: Circumstances leave no other option — one is compelled into X. Formal, passive framing; the subject is the unwilling party.

examples.en: The typhoon forced us to postpone the trip.
Some people are forced to return home over visa problems.
A budget shortfall forced us to scale back the plan.

caution: The passive を余儀なくされる has the subject forced by circumstances into X; don't mix it with the active を余儀なくさせる, where circumstances force it upon someone else.

peg.ja: 全軍は、撤退を余儀なくされた。
peg.romaji: Zengun wa, tettai o yoginaku sareta.
peg.en: The entire army was forced to retreat.
peg.source: 進撃の巨人 — エルヴィン

nuance: An emphatic identity claim — it is precisely X and nothing else. Often used to assert the true cause or essence of something.

examples.en: This success is nothing other than the result of everyone's effort.
The daily overtime is nothing other than the fault of the staff shortage.
This result is nothing other than the accumulation of daily effort.

caution: It asserts 'is precisely X and nothing else', so the X should be the true cause/essence — don't weaken it to a mere 'is X'; the emphatic identity is the whole point.

peg.ja: この勝利は、計算の結果にほかならない。
peg.romaji: Kono shōri wa, keisan no kekka ni hokanaranai.
peg.en: This victory is nothing other than the result of calculation.
peg.source: デスノート — 夜神月

connection: plain form / N + とあって
nuance: Because of a special or notable circumstance, a natural consequence follows. Reserved for out-of-the-ordinary situations, reportorial in tone.

examples.en: Given that it was a long holiday, the station was packed with travelers.
Given that it's my first time living abroad, I'm a little nervous.
Given that it was the last day of the long weekend, the up-bound bullet trains were full.

caution: Don't confuse とあって with だけあって: とあって is reportorial ('given this special situation, naturally...'), used for out-of-the-ordinary circumstances, while だけあって praises a result as fitting a cause.

peg.ja: 決勝戦とあって、会場は満員だった。
peg.romaji: Kesshōsen to atte, kaijō wa man'in datta.
peg.en: Given that it was the final, the arena was packed.
peg.source: ハイキュー!! — 烏養繋心

nuance: Once you reach a certain elevated level or stage, things naturally become different. Implies the noun marks a high threshold.

examples.en: When it comes to December, the town suddenly turns hectic.
When it comes to rent in the city center, it's not something you can pay easily.
When it comes to the busy season, even taking a day off is hard.

caution: ともなると needs the noun to mark a genuinely high level or stage (プロともなると) — don't use it for an ordinary, low-threshold situation where になると alone suffices.

peg.ja: 頂点ともなると、見える景色が違う。
peg.romaji: Chōten to mo naru to, mieru keshiki ga chigau.
peg.en: When it comes to the summit, the view is different.
peg.source: ハイキュー!! — 及川徹

nuance: You wind up stuck in an unwelcome situation, usually through bad luck or someone else's doing. Casual and rueful.

examples.en: I missed the last train and ended up stuck at the station all night.
My documents were incomplete, so I ended up having to go to the ward office twice.
Lured by the low price, I signed and ended up regretting it.

caution: はめになる always ends in an unwelcome, sorry outcome you got stuck with — don't use it for a good or neutral result, or as a plain 'became' (ことになる).

peg.ja: 油断した結果、逃げ回るはめになった。
peg.romaji: Yudan shita kekka, nigemawaru hame ni natta.
peg.en: Off my guard, I ended up running for my life.
peg.source: 銀魂 — 坂田銀時
